PNG画像を読み込むだけで任意コード実行につながりうる脆弱性が、画像処理ライブラリlibpngで2件修正されています。いずれもCVSSスコアはHighで、修正版は1.6.56。  特にCVE-2026-33416はlibpng 1.2.1以降に約25年間潜在していたUse-After-Freeで、攻撃者が細工したPNGファイルを読み込ませることでヒープ破壊を引き起こせます。 glibc tcacheのように解放済みメモリが決定論的に再利用されるアロケータ環境かつPIE無効の条件下で、RCE（任意コード実行）のPoCも実証済みとされており、組み込み機器やレガシーサーバーでは特にリスクが高い状況。 細工されたPNGファイルはPNG規格に完全準拠しているため、WAFによるフィルタリングでは正規のPNGと区別がつかない点も厄介です。  libpngはあらゆるプラットフォームで間接的に利用されていることが多く、直接依存していなくても他のライブラリやフレームワーク経由で影響を受ける可能性があります。依存関係の確認と早めのアップデートを。  【概要とポイント】  ・libpng公式がGitHub Security Advisoryを公開。CVE-2026-33416（CVSS 7.5）とCVE-2026-33636（CVSS 7.6）の2件  ・CVE-2026-33416：png_set_tRNSおよびpng_set_PLTEにおけるポインタエイリアシングに起因するUse-After-Free。png_structとpng_infoが単一のヒープバッファを共有し、一方を解放しても他方のポインタがダングリング状態で残る。攻撃者はPNGのtRNSチャンク値を通じて解放済みメモリに書き込む値を制御可能とされる  ・CVE-2026-33636：ARM/AArch64 Neon最適化のパレット展開処理における境界外読み書き。ループの最終反復で入力ピクセル残量を検証せず、行バッファ手前のメモリにアクセスしてしまう。ヒープ内容の漏洩やプロセスクラッシュが確認されており、対象はlibpng 1.6.36〜1.6.55をNeon有効でビルドしたARM環境に限定  ・CVE-2026-33636のみの暫定対策として、-DPNG_ARM_NEON_OPT=0を指定してハードウェア最適化を無効にしたリビルドで回避可能。ただし画像処理性能が低下するとのこと  ・CVE-2026-33416にはワークアラウンドの記載がなく、ライブラリ更新が唯一の対策  github.com/pnggroup/libpng/s…

Everyone is talking about the Anthropic "leak" like it’s a sophisticated cyberattack.  It wasn't. It was a missing line in a .npmignore file.  Yesterday, Anthropic accidentally shipped the full source code for Claude Code to the public npm registry. 512,000 lines of TypeScript, 1,900 files, and the entire "agentic harness" that makes the model actually useful.  +1  If you're a Product Owner or Tech Leader, stop looking at the drama and start looking at the engineering. This is the most expensive masterclass in AI architecture we’ve ever seen.  Here are the 3 biggest takeaways for anyone building AI products:  The "Harness" is the Moat: The leak confirms what many of us suspected—the model (Claude 3.5/4) is just the engine. The real magic is the "Strict Write Discipline" and the three-layer memory architecture (MEMORY.md pointers) that prevents context entropy. That’s how they stop agents from hallucinating during long dev sessions.  KAIROS is the Future: The code is littered with references to an unreleased background daemon called "KAIROS." It’s an always-on agent that performs "memory consolidation" while you sleep. We’re moving from reactive chat boxes to persistent background coworkers.+2  The "Bun" Factor: They aren't using Node.js. They’re built on the Bun runtime for raw performance. Even at the frontier, every millisecond of latency in the loop matters for agentic UX. The Irony: The codebase literally contains a feature called "Undercover Mode" designed to prevent Claude from leaking internal secrets in commits. It worked perfectly. But it couldn't stop a human from pushing a debug .map file to npm.  Security isn't just about protecting the model weights anymore; it's about the boring stuff in your CI/CD pipeline.  Is your team auditing your build artifacts as closely as your code, or are we all just one misconfigured config file away from a "forced" open-source release?

Open Source Breach Hits AI Startup Mercor  Mercor disclosed a cyberattack linked to a compromised open source project used in its systems   The breach traces to LiteLLM a widely used library downloaded millions of times per day   Malicious code was inserted into LiteLLM packages and remained live for about 40 minutes before removal   The compromised versions targeted credentials including cloud keys SSH keys and database passwords  The payload was designed to extract and exfiltrate sensitive data from affected environments   An extortion group claimed responsibility and shared samples of stolen data including internal communications   Mercor has not confirmed whether customer or contractor data was accessed as investigations continue   The incident highlights risks from supply chain attacks where one compromised dependency can affect many companies

In 2023, I started a project aimed at sourcing and analysing Cyber attacks across Africa. The primary objective was to understand attack patterns within the region and leverage these insights to build actionable threat intelligence. However, an issue I faced was the limited availability of reliable data, largely due to the underreporting of Cyber incidents across many African countries.   This raises questions about transparency and regulatory enforcement. Using Nigeria as an example, I understand that there is a regulation mandating organizations to report cyber attacks but I am not certain about it’s enforcement and awareness.   This lack of visibility creates a big concern: How can organisations, government and security professionals learn from past incidents if they are not adequately reported?   Alternatively, does this underreporting suggest that our systems and infrastructure are highly secure, or does it point to gaps in disclosure, and accountability?

Zero-day discovery in OSS is now wildly easier. Defenders are already behind. And when the chaos hits, no AI engine is saving you: That's a human problem.  The idea that "AI will replace security teams" is one of the most dangerous myths floating around right now, and the people pushing it clearly don't understand how any of this actually works.  Finding zero-days in OSS codebases has gotten dramatically easier. That's not a good thing for defenders. The wave of exploits that follows is going to create real chaos, the kind that needs human expertise to untangle, not an automated pipeline.  OSS developers are about to be absolutely buried. We're talking thousands of open security tickets, constant patch cycles, regression testing on physical hardware that has to actually be in the room. For unpaid maintainers, that's not sustainable. A lot of them are just going to walk away.  And after everything we've seen with supply chain attacks, you'd think people would know better, but AI CLI tooling has the same problem. You can compromise someone's machine through a maliciously crafted file sitting in a GitHub repo. Same attack surface, new wrapper.  Those "AI-powered" AppSec platforms being sold as autonomous defenders? Most of them are still largely built around OWASP Top 10. That's a checklist from a different era. Meanwhile the actual exploits being found in the wild are in the infrastructure those tools run on. The gap between what these platforms cover and what's really being exploited is getting wider every day.  Human defenders aren't going anywhere, but honestly, what we actually need is fewer products that bolt security on at the end and more teams building it in from the start. Security-aware development, security by design. That's the only way to stop digging the hole deeper.

One of the greatest failures of this war has been the absolute devastation of American bases in the Gulf by Iran. 13 bases were left uninhabitable and several more were damaged. It has now been revealed that the issue of the vulnerability of these bases had been raised repeatedly in the past and ignored:  1) Frank McKenzie who led Central Command raised it during the first Trump term. Those who followed him warned Biden and Trump repeatedly.   2) They proposed to build a network of bases further west, deeper inside Saudi Arabia. They argued that bases further west in Saudi Arabia would have been significantly farther and fewer Iranian missiles and drones could actually reach them, because range is a limiting factor even for Iran's arsenal. Those that could reach would arrive with less fuel, potentially less accuracy, and less payload. Warning times would increase, giving defenders more time to respond and aircraft more time to scramble or shelter  3) The Biden administration was prioritizing the Pacific/China competition and didn't want a heavier Middle East footprint. The Trump administration, despite initiating this war, also didn't pursue the western basing network even though they had time to prepare.  4) Satellite photos of Prince Sultan throughout March show the E-3 parked in the same location on multiple days before it was struck. That's not a minor operational lapse — that's a fundamental violation of basic force protection principles during an active conflict with a adversary that has demonstrated both the will and capability to strike that exact base. An E-3 AWACS is not a fighter jet. It's an enormous, slow, distinctive aircraft that can't hide easily. Everyone in the military knows it's a high-value target. The entire Iranian military knows it's a high-value target.  5) The bases were not properly hardened either. Hegseth claimed before the war started that the military had taken steps to "fortify and disperse forces" and that commanders were working to avoid predictable patterns. The satellite imagery directly contradicts this. The E-3 was parked in the same spot on multiple days throughout March, which is about as predictable a pattern as you can have.  This was a foreseeable vulnerability was flagged repeatedly, ignored by two successive administrations for bureaucratic and strategic reasons, and is now being paid for in damaged aircraft and wounded troops during an active war.

FCMB: Sophisticated API exploitation resulted in the successful siphoning of ₦677 million from a ₦3.5 billion fraudulent attempt.  Sterling Bank: A critical middleware vulnerability enabled the exfiltration of sensitive PII for over 900,000 customers.  Remita: A massive cloud misconfiguration exposed 3TB of archival data, including transaction logs and infrastructure blueprints.  Here is a clean technical breakdown of these incidents:  1. FCMB: The ₦3.5 Billion Heist  This was a logic based exploitation of the bank's digital transaction pipeline    Attackers identified a flaw in the API reconciliation layer, specifically involving the Payattitude integration  By exploiting this vulnerability, hackers initiated transactions that the system validated as successful even though the source accounts were unfunded. This is known as a Zero Balance or Double Spend exploit.  While the system eventually flagged the anomaly at the ₦3.5 billion mark, the latency in the bank's real-time fraud monitoring allowed ₦677 million to be successfully routed to mule accounts and withdrawn before the kill switch was activated.  2. Sterling Bank: The 900k+ Record Exfiltration  This event was kinda like a Network Intrusion targeted at customer identity data, allegedly carried out by the threat actor ByteToBreach.  The breach targeted a critical vulnerability in the Oracle WebLogic Server. This middleware sits between the public facing applications and the bank’s private databases.  Attackers bypassed authentication to extract roughly 2.2 GB of data.  The data contained Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for over 900,000 customers, including names, contact details, and internal Customer Information File (CIF) numbers. This data is highly valuable for "Social Engineering 2.0, where scammers use real account details to trick victims into revealing OTPs or other lateral valuable infos  3. Remita: The 3TB S3 Infrastructure Exposure  This was a Critical Cloud Misconfiguration representing one of the largest infrastructure level exposures in the Nigerian fintech space  A massive Amazon S3 Bucket (Cloud Storage) was left in a Public Read state. This meant the data was accessible to anyone with the endpoint URL, requiring no hacking tools or passwords to download   The volume 3 Terabytes indicates an entire archival Data Lake was exposed. This typically includes millions of individual files and logs accumulated over years  800GB+ of KYC Documents, Massive troves of sensitive personal data, including Passports, Government IDs, Bank Statements, and Utility Bills  Core Databases: Full exports of MySQL and Postgres databases, including three primary databases and over 35,000+ password hashes  The Master Keys: Exposure of Government HSM (Hardware Security Module) keys, which are used to encrypt and authorize high-level financial transactions  Developer Blueprints: Source code, Docker registries, and GitKraken-to-S3 backups, providing a literal how-to guide for attackers to find further vulnerabilities in the system's logic  The exposure included transaction archives, RRR (Remita Retrieval Reference) metadata, and internal system logs. Most dangerously, logs of this size often leak secrets such as API keys and session tokens, which provide a roadmap for attackers to move laterally into other connected financial systems  what can we do

✅ 미국에서 2025년 자동차 절도 건수가 전년 대비 25% 급감하며 65만 9,880건을 기록한 것으로 알려졌습니다. 이는 경찰 대응 강화와 완성차 업체들의 보안 취약점 개선 효과가 반영된 결과로 평가되고 있는데요.  그럼에도 불구하고 차량 절도는 여전히 빈번하게 발생하고 있으며, 약 48초마다 한 대꼴로 차량이 도난 당하고 있는 상황이죠.  이 가운데 도둑들이 가장 선호하는 차량 1위는 현대 아반떼로 나타났다. 아반떼는 총 2만 1,732대가 도난되며 ‘가장 훔치고 싶은 차’라는 별명을 얻은 것으로 알려졌는데요.  2위와 3위는 각각 혼다 어코드와 현대 쏘나타가 차지하며, 비교적 대중적인 세단이 주요 타깃이 되는 경향을 보였습니다.  픽업트럭 시장에서는 쉐보레 실버라도 1500이 포드 F-150보다 60% 더 높은 도난율을 기록해 눈길을 끌었죠.  최근 차량 절도 수법도 진화하고 있는 것으로 알려졌는데, 키 복제 방식에서 나아가, 차량 헤드라이트 배선을 통해 시스템에 침입하는 ‘헤드라이트 해킹’까지 등장한 것으로 알려졌습니다.  전문가들은 경보장치, GPS 추적기 등 기본적인 보안 장치 사용을 권장하고 있으며, 수동변속기 차량이 여전히 가장 강력한 도난 방지 수단으로 꼽히네요.  결국, 가장 훔치고 싶은 차 1위에 선정되어버린 아반떼, 여러분은 어떻게 생각하나요?  *이해를 돕기 위한 ai 이미지가 포함되어 있습니다.  @maylongallery @cityano  네이버 남차카페 - 자동차 제보  ✔ 매일 빠른 최신 자동차 소식은? ▶ @motorsjason 팔로우+즐겨찾기!  #자동차 #car

최근 원/달러 환율 상승(고환율)과 삼성전자 노조 리스크의 연관성은 단순한 ‘우연의 일치’가 아니라, 한국 경제의 구조적 취약점(반도체 수출 의존 + 지정학 리스크 + 내부 노사 갈등)이 맞물린 ‘복합 리스크’로 작용하고 있습니다. 2026년 3월 말 현재, 두 현상이 동시에 고조되면서 **국가 경제에 ‘이중·삼중 타격’**을 주고 있어요.  삼성전자 노조 리스크: 초기업노조·전국삼성전자노조 등 주도로 5월 총파업(5월 21일~6월 7일 예정) 준비 중. 쟁의권 투표 93.1% 찬성.ca1b11b37cad 핵심 쟁점은 초과이익성과급(OPI) 상한 폐지 (현재 연봉 50% 캡). 회사 측은 ‘업계 최고 특별 보상’ 제안했지만 노조는 영구적 제도 변경을 고수하며 협상 중단 → 파업 예고.  파업 시 반도체(DS) 부문 생산 차질이 핵심. 평택·기흥 공장 중심으로 메모리·HBM 라인 멈추면 예상 손실 9~10조 원 (18일간).  방향 메커니즘 구체적 영향 노조 리스크 → 환율 상승 압력  반도체 생산 차질 → 수출 감소 → 무역수지 악화 → 달러 공급 부족  반도체 수출 비중 37% (최근 월 250억 달러). 파업으로 월 50억 달러 수출 감소 추정 → 달러 유입 줄어 환율 추가 상승.  환율 상승 → 노조 리스크 증폭 고환율·고유가 → 물가 상승 → 노조 ‘성과급 더 달라’ 요구 강화  이미 반도체 슈퍼사이클로 실적 폭발 중인데, 환율 상승으로 원가(수입 장비·원자재) 부담 ↑ → 노조 “회사 돈 많이 버는데 왜 상한 두나” 주장에 힘 실림.  ... 출처 그록4.2  현대기아차그룹이 보스턴다이내믹스로 강하게 올라올 때, 이때다 싶어 나온 노조의 움직임으로 주가의 탄력성은 낮아지고 그로인해 외국계 공격적 성향 자금이 강하게 못들어오면서 지지부진하지만 워낙 노조가 유명했던지라 제한적이나..  삼성그룹은 다릅니다.. 삼성그룹의 노조가 파업을 하고 장기화가 된다면.. 그 파급력은 삼성전자에 국한되는게 아니고 우리나라 대한민국 모든 외화벌이 대기업군 뿐 아니라 모든기업에 대한 돌발 리스크가 확대되면서..   외화벌이 기업들 자체가 외화를 벌어들이지 못한다면 2월말 기준 외환보유고 4,000억달러는 순식간에 희석될 수 있을듯하다..  또한 휴전으로 인한 코리아디스카운트를 넘어 휴전 이외 추가되는 새로운 개념의 글로벌 코리아디스카운트가 생길 수 있다고 보여지기에..   삼성그룹 또한 반도체사업부문 힘들다고 DS부문과 DX부문의 괴리감은 이해하시어 원만하게 타협하시길 바라옵니다.. 국가와 나라를 생각하시어 양쪽 모두 좋게 마무리 해주시면 감사드리겠습니다..

sample_tweets.text: $TSLA 🇰🇷 대한민국 국토교통부는 테슬라 소유주들에게 FSD를 불법적으로 활성화하려는 시도는 형사 범죄라고 엄중히 경고했습니다.   이번 조치는 테슬라 코리아가 무단 활성화 시도와 관련된 소프트웨어 취약점을 발견하고 이를 중대한 사이버 보안 위협으로 공식 보고한 데 따른 것입니다.    교통부에 따르면 해외에서 일부 차량 소유주들이 외부 "탈옥" 장치를 이용해 테슬라의 소프트웨어 잠금을 해제하고 FSD (완전 자율 주행)를 활성화한 사례가 여러 건 발생했습니다.   이러한 방법과 오픈 소스 코드가 온라인에 유포되고 있는 상황에서 한국 당국은 국내 운전자들이 허가받지 않은 차량에 동일한 개조를 시도할 가능성을 우려하고 있습니다.   현재 한국에서는 미국에서 생산된 모델 S, 모델 X, 그리고 사이버트럭에만 FSD (완전 자율 주행)가 법적으로 허용되어 있습니다.   출처: 코리아중강데일리

Kaspersky discovers CrystalX RAT - a unique MaaS combining traditional spyware with "prankware" features designed to troll victims. Active since January 2026, this Go-based malware targets credentials, cryptocurrency, and includes anti-analysis evasion.  Technical breakdown: • Encryption: zlib compression + ChaCha20 with hardcoded 32-byte key and 12-byte nonce • Anti-analysis: VM detection, MITM checks, debug loops, and stealth patches for AMSI/ETW bypass • Stealer: Extracts Steam/Discord/Telegram credentials, uses ChromeElevator utility dropped to %TEMP%\svc[rndInt].exe • Clipper: Injects malicious extension into Chrome/Edge via %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Edge\ExtSvc directory • Unique "Rofl" panel: Desktop rotation, mouse remapping, cursor shake, BSoD simulation, peripheral disruption  C2 communication via WebSocket protocol sends data in JSON plaintext. VNC functionality enables remote desktop control with input blocking capabilities.  Hunt for suspicious files in %TEMP% matching pattern svc[digits].exe and monitor Chrome DevTools CDP protocol usage for unauthorized extension loading.  #DFIR_Radar

Supply chain attack compromises popular Axios npm package. Malicious versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 deployed cross-platform RAT after maintainer account takeover, impacting macOS, Windows, and Linux systems globally.  Technical details: • Compromised packages: axios-1.14.1.tgz, axios-0.30.4.tgz, and dependency plain-crypto-js-4.2.1.tgz • Multi-stage payload chain: setup.js → platform-specific RATs (system.bat for Windows, ld.py for Linux, com.apple.act.mond for macOS) • C2 infrastructure: sfrclak[.]com:8000 (142[.]11[.]206[.]73) with platform-specific endpoints • Anti-forensics: Removes installation artifacts and replaces package metadata post-deployment • Windows persistence via C:\ProgramData\wt.exe and system.bat batch script  DFIR artifacts: • Monitor npm install logs and package-lock.json for affected versions • Hunt for executables in C:\ProgramData\wt.exe and suspicious batch files • Check network connections to sfrclak[.]com and callnrwise[.]com domains • Review PowerShell execution logs for obfuscated scripts (Sophos: Troj/PSAgent-CN)  Immediate actions: Audit all Axios installations, check SHA256 hashes against IOC list, and scan for C2 beaconing. Full IOC table with 16+ hashes available in report.  #DFIR_Radar

Supply chain attack: Attackers compromised axios maintainer accounts, bypassing CI/CD to inject malware into versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. Over 300M weekly downloads affected across all platforms.  Attack methodology: • Attackers hijacked Jason Saayman's npm/GitHub accounts, changed email to ifstap@proton.me • Created clean plain-crypto-js@4.2.0 package first, then malicious @4.2.1 version 18hrs later to evade detection • Bypassed normal GitHub Actions CI/CD, manually released poisoned axios versions via npm CLI • RAT dropper (setup.js) detects OS and deploys platform-specific payloads to /Library/Caches/com.apple.act.mond (macOS), /tmp/ld.py (Linux), %PROGRAMDATA%\wt.exe (Windows) • Self-destructs after execution: deletes setup.js, replaces package.json with clean version  DFIR artifacts: • Malicious versions: axios@1.14.1 (SHA1: 2553649f2322049666871cea80a5d0d6adc700ca), axios@0.30.4 (SHA1: d6f3f62fd3b9f5432f5782b62d8cfd5247d5ee71) • C2 domains: sfrclak.com, callnrwise.com (142.11.206.73) • User-Agent: mozilla/4.0 (compatible; msie 8.0; windows nt 5.1; trident/4.0)  Check npm dependencies for affected versions and hunt for platform-specific backdoor files at the locations above. Full IOCs and detection commands in the report.  #DFIR_Radar

Claude Code Leakage: A Lesson from Human Operation  @bcherny's latest response confirms what many suspected: humans played the main role in this incident. The source leak came from a manual deployment step, not from AI-generated code.  We have long debated how much human oversight belongs in AI workflows. This error reminds us that the question cuts both ways.   Human involvement can introduce risk just as easily as it prevents it. Cherny's proposed fix is telling: "More automation, and having Claude check the results."  The lesson here is practical. Machines excel at repeatable tasks. Humans excel at judgment calls. When we blur that line, mistakes happen.  So as AI capabilities grow, the question becomes: where exactly should we draw the line?

Ważny i niebezpieczny artykuł w prestiżowym magazynie The New Yorker, znanym ze swoich umiarkowanych stanowisk wobec spraw globalnych, którego łamy – jak się podkreśla – nie zostały wykupione przez służby wywiadowcze, publikuje istotny tekst o przenikaniu agentów Mossad, CIA oraz innych służb do pracy dziennikarskiej. Agenci tych tajnych struktur stają się dziennikarzami, swobodnie poruszając się po korytarzach Białego Domu, Kongresu, Pentagonu i ośrodków decyzyjnych. W ten sposób przełamują barierę strachu u ludzi – bo rozmówcy chętnie rozmawiają z dziennikarzami, podczas gdy odmawiają rozmów z agentami wywiadu. Wraz z wysunięciem się na pierwszy plan portalu Axios, który publikuje „ekskluzywne” doniesienia i codzienne raporty o amerykańsko-izraelskiej wojnie przeciwko Iranowi – podobnie jak wcześniej w przypadku Gazy – ważne jest umieszczenie tego zjawiska we właściwym kontekście i uznanie go za część szerszej operacji informacyjnej. Śledztwo przeprowadzone przez MintPress News, autorstwa Alan MacLeod, opublikowane w październiku 2024 roku, ujawniło, że Barak Ravid – obecnie czołowy korespondent w Axios oraz analityk polityczny i ekspert ds. polityki zagranicznej w CNN – wcześniej służył w izraelskiej jednostce Unit 8200, odpowiedzialnej za masową inwigilację, cyberwojnę i kontrowersyjne operacje. Raport wskazuje, że jego publikacje konsekwentnie powielają narracje rządów USA i Izraela, przedstawiając oficjalne przekazy jako „ekskluzywne” informacje. Dochodzenie pokazuje, że nie jest to odosobniony przypadek – wymieniane są inne osoby powiązane wcześniej z jednostką 8200, obecne w głównych mediach zachodnich i sektorze technologicznym. Wśród nich znajdują się m.in. Shahar Peled, Tal Heinrich, która pracowała również w CNN, zanim została rzeczniczką Benjamin Netanyahu, oraz Tamar Michaelis. Według raportu ta sieć odgrywa kluczową rolę w budowaniu poparcia dla amerykańsko-izraelskich wojen, upiększaniu operacji wojskowych oraz marginalizowaniu narracji z Azji Zachodniej. Wnioski wskazują, że granica między dziennikarstwem a operacjami wywiadowczymi w praktyce się zatarła, przekształcając całe segmenty zachodnich mediów w wyraźne narzędzia wpływu państwa izraelskiego. Na zdjęciu znajduje się agentka Mossadu Tamar Michaelis, która została starszą producentką ds. relacji Izrael/Palestyna w CNN. Uważa się, że mogła wyrazić zgodę na niedawne pobicie i zatrzymanie ekipy CNN przez izraelskie siły, ponieważ materiał nagłośnił ataki osadników na palestyńskich cywilów – co stoi w sprzeczności z zadaniem przypisywanym jej przez Mossad, polegającym na promowaniu wyłącznie oficjalnej narracji izraelskiej.

Cybersecurity nerds this week after experiencing 50 supply chain attacks last week

There is a project on GitHub called Axios.  Axios is extremely popular. It is used by millions upon millions of applications.  Axios is a programming library that helps your JavaScript code make HTTP/S requests (communicate with websites).  In simple terms, if you're a programmer doing something with JavaScript, and want to do stuff that communicates with a website in literally any capacity, people heavily recommend using Axios due to its simplicity. Using Axios you don't have to reinvent the wheel and do a bunch of work. All you need to do is import Axios into your code and you're off to the races.  Someone (currently unknown) compromised Axios (currently unknown how) to deliver malware to people. When someone updates or installs Axios, Axios itself contains malware.  What the malware does is (currently) unknown, but it is being reversed engineered by probably every malware analyst on the planet at this moment. In a few hours more details will emerge. Information is being exchanged in real time on social media and private communication platforms as I write this.  Due to the size and popularity of Axios, it is unknown how many are impacted, it could be millions, it could be thousands, or if we're lucky, only hundreds of people or organizations will be impacted.  If this is absolute worst case scenario, millions of organizations across the planet have been infected with malware which (currently) we do not understand. However, the likelihood of this is low. It appears Axios being compromised was detected quickly, potentially within minutes (or hours) of it being compromised to deliver malware. Additionally, the likelihood of every single Axios user updating Axios as soon as it was compromised to deliver malware is astronomically low. It is basically zero.  The impact from Axios being compromised is devastating, the fallout from this will be a massive headache. This is unironically a malware nuclear missile and will likely be studied in the future.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-04-01 18:07 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 오픈소스 생태계의 신뢰를 악용하는 **Supply Chain Attack**이 핵심 공격 벡터로 부상하고 있으며, 특히 `Axios` 및 `LiteLLM`과 같은 널리 사용되는 라이브러리를 타겟팅하여 개발 환경을 침투하는 사례가 급증하고 있습니다. 또한, AI 에이전트 및 클라우드 인프라의 **Over-privileged** 문제를 악용하여 데이터 유출을 시도하는 공격이 정교화되고 있습니다. 공격자들은 `ClickFix`와 같은 사회공학적 기법을 통해 사용자 상호작용을 유도하거나, 랜섬웨어 탐지 우회 및 데이터 파괴를 목적으로 하는 공격을 지속적으로 수행하고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **UNC1069 (Google Threat Intelligence Group)**: 북한 배후의 금전적 목적 그룹으로, `Axios` npm 패키지 공급망 공격을 통해 악성 코드를 유포함.
- **TeamPCP (Unit 42)**: 클라우드 및 SaaS 인프라를 타겟으로 하는 공격 그룹으로, 최근 `Vect` 랜섬웨어 그룹과 파트너십을 맺고 보안 인프라를 무기화함.
- **Larva-26002 (AhnLab ASEC)**: 관리 부실 MS-SQL 서버를 타겟으로 스캐너를 설치하고 랜섬웨어를 유포하는 활동 지속.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Axios npm 공급망 공격**: 북한 배후 그룹에 의한 공격으로, 국내 개발 환경 및 기업의 오픈소스 의존성 관리에 직접적인 위협이 됨 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5742)).
- **과도한 광고 앱 유포**: 안랩 ASEC은 구글 플레이스토어 및 애플 앱스토어에서 정부 정책/학습 앱으로 위장한 과대 광고 앱 유포 사례를 확인 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/93141/)).
- **국세청 사칭 피싱**: 국세청 세무조사를 사칭한 피싱 메일을 통해 백도어 악성코드를 유포하는 캠페인 지속 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5741)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2025-71279**: Multiple/Unspecified Products, CVSS 9.8 (Critical), 악용 여부 확인 필요 ([CVEarity](https://twitter.com/CVEarity/status/2039259903882379618)).
